PROOF OF SERVICE OF OF ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA SERVED FEB-13-2013, MAIL AS TO DEFENDANT SMITH, DAMIEN
PROOF OF SERVICE OF PETITION FOR FORFEITURE IN REM FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ALTHOUGH THE MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION, AND THE DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION REPRESENT THAT THE DISCOVERY WAS SERVED ON THE CLAIMANT ON JUNE 9, 2011, THOSE REPRESENTATIONS ARE INCONSISTENT WITH THE PROOF OF SERVICE EXECUTED BY CHLOE T. MOSQUEDA. MOREOVER, WHILE THE MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION STATES THAT "[T]O DATE, NO RESPONSES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM CLAIMANT," THAT IS NOT WHAT THE SUPPORTING DECLARATION SAYS. IN THE DECLARATION, COUNSEL REPRESENTS ONLY THAT "[T]O DATE, PETITIONER HAS NOT RECEIVED COMPLETE ANSWERS TO THESE DISCOVERY REQUESTS." DECLARATION OF PAMELA A. UNDERWOOD AT PARA. 3, EMPHASIS ADDED. BECAUSE THE SUBSTANTIVE AND PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR MOTIONS COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY DIFFER, DEPENDING UPON WHETHER INADEQUATE ANSWERS WERE SERVED, OR NO ANSWERS AT ALL WERE PROVIDED, AND BECAUSE THE PAPERS SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION ARE IN CONFLICT ON THIS ISSUE, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: RICHARD GROTCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED

Mtn To Compel Answers To First Set Of Special Interoggs
Off Calendar Feb-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pamela A. Underwood, Esq., Asst. District Attorney, Tel. 415.554.9544, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was unopposed. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Special Interrogatories is granted. The motion was unopposed. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: James Watson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported

Ntc Of Motion To Compel Answers
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Unopposed Motion To Compel Answers is DENIED without prejudice. Although the memorandum in support of the motion, and the declaration in support of the motion represent that the discovery was served on the claimant on June 9, 2011, those representations are inconsistent with the proof of service executed by Chloe T. Mosqueda. Moreover, while the memorandum in support of the motion states that "[t]o date, no responses have been received from Claimant," that is not what the supporting declaration says. In the declaration, counsel represents only that "[t]o date, Petitioner has not received complete answers to these discovery requests." Declaration of Pamela A. Underwood at para. 3, emphasis added. Because the substantive and procedural requirements for motions compelling responses to discovery differ, depending upon whether inadequate answers were served, or no answers at all were provided, and because the papers submitted in support of this motion are in conflict on this issue, the motion is DENIED without prejudice. Court to prepare order. Judge Pro Tem: Richard Grotch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
